Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendants Corvorn Llc, Jeremiah Cullinane And Michael J. Mamones Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. EDWARD F. MITCHELL, ESQ. of Law Offices of Edward F. Mitchell, Tel No. 415-265-4220 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. MATTHEW K. YAN, ESQ. of Moscone Emblidge & Otis LLP, Tel No. 415-362-3599 appearing for Defendants Jeremiah Cullinane, Michael Mamone and Corvon LLC.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JEREMIAH CULLINANE, MICHAEL MAMONE AND CORVORN LLC'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation pending the conclusion of the arbitration proceedings is granted. Plaintiff's claims are covered by the arbitration provisions of the contracts that she claims have been breached. All of plaintiff's arguments opposing arbitration lack merit. Because plaintiff's claims are based on the three operating agreements and those agreements require that all claims arising out of the agreements be arbitrated, application of the arbitration provisions does not depend on whether plaintiff is still a member of the LLCs. Plaintiff has not shown that there is a risk of inconsistent results if she is compelled to arbitrate her claims. Nor has plaintiff shown that she should not be required to bear the costs of arbitration. If "a party seeks to invalidate an arbitration agreement on the ground that arbitration would be prohibitively expensive, that party bears the burden of showing the likelihood of incurring such costs." (Green Tree Financial Corp.-Ala. v. Randolph (2000) 531 U.S. 79, 91-92.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 (302/HK)
